topic.name: Classic Video Games
topic.description: The documents discuss various classic video games, their development, gameplay mechanics, and historical significance.

1: Super Mario Bros. : The Lost Levels is a 1986 side-scrolling, platformer action game developed and published by Nintendo as the sequel to the 1985 Super Mario Bros.
The Lost Levels also introduces setbacks like poison mushroom power-ups, counterproductive level warps, and mid-air wind gusts.
2: Marvel vs. Capcom 2 is a crossover fighting game developed and published by Capcom.
Originally released in Japanese arcades in 2000, the game received ports to the Dreamcast, PlayStation 2, PlayStation 3, Xbox, Xbox 360, and iOS devices over the span of twelve years.
3: Jetpac Refuelled is a shooter video game developed by Rare and published by Microsoft Studios.
The game is the fourth and final instalment of the Jetman series and a remake of Ultimate Play the Game's 1983 ZX Spectrum game, Jetpac.
4: Mega Man 2 is an action-platform game developed and published by Capcom for the Nintendo Entertainment System.
The game features graphical and gameplay changes from the first Mega Man game, many of which have remained throughout the series.
5: Dragon Ball Z : Attack of the Saiyans is a video game based on the manga and anime series Dragon Ball for the Nintendo DS.
The game is a turn-based RPG that allows players to take on the role of six characters from the series.
6: Wonder Boy III : The Dragon's Trap is a platforming and action-adventure video game developed by Westone.
The game takes place immediately after the events of Wonder Boy in Monster Land.
7: Castle Crashers is a 2D beat 'em up video game developed by The Behemoth.
The Xbox 360 version was released on August 27, 2008 via Xbox Live Arcade as part of the Xbox Live Summer of Arcade.
8: Wario World is a platform beat 'em up game developed by Treasure and published by Nintendo for the GameCube.
The game centers on Wario and his quest to regain his treasure and his castle from Black Jewel.
9: Super Mario World is a platform video game developed and published by Nintendo as a pack-in launch title for the Super Nintendo Entertainment System.
The game centers on the quest of Mario and Luigi to save Dinosaur Land from Bowser.
10: Mega Man : Dr. Wily's Revenge is a platform video game by Capcom for the Nintendo Game Boy.
The game continues the adventures of the android hero Mega Man as he once again confronts the evil Dr. Wily.

topic.name: Legal Frameworks and Rights
topic.description: The documents discuss various legal frameworks, rights, and procedures related to criminal justice, constitutional law, and human rights in different jurisdictions.

Document 1: The United States Constitution contains several provisions regarding the law of criminal procedure.
Several of these rights regulate pre-trial procedure.
Document 2: The Internal Security Act (ISA) of Singapore is a statute that grants the executive power to enforce preventive detention.
Before a person can be detained under the ISA by the Minister for Home Affairs, the President must be satisfied that such detention is necessary for the purposes of national security.
Document 3: Article 14 of the Constitution of the Republic of Singapore guarantees to Singapore citizens the rights to freedom of speech and expression.
The enjoyment of these rights may be restricted by laws imposed by the Parliament of Singapore.
Document 4: The Optional Protocol to the Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination against Women establishes complaint and inquiry mechanisms.
The Protocol has led to a number of decisions against member states on issues such as domestic violence.
Document 5: The Canadian Afghan detainee issue concerns whether or not the Government of Canada and/or the Canadian Forces had knowledge about alleged abusive treatment of detainees.
If the allegations of torture are true it would mean Canada is guilty of war crimes.
Document 6: Exclusion of judicial review has been attempted by the Parliament of Singapore to protect the exercise of executive power.
In contrast, Singapore cases suggest that ouster clauses cannot prevent the High Court from exercising supervisory jurisdiction.
Document 7: The Bahrain Thirteen are thirteen Bahraini opposition leaders arrested in connection with their role in the national uprising.
The trial was 'one of the most prominent' before the National Safety Court.
Document 8: The Constitution of the Republic of Singapore Tribunal was established in 1994 pursuant to Article 100 of the Constitution.
Questions referred to the Tribunal may concern the validity of enacted laws or of bills that have not yet been passed by Parliament.
Document 9: The doctrine of legitimate expectation in Singapore protects both procedural and substantive rights.
To derogate from the representation may amount to an abuse of power or unfairness.
Document 10: The International Criminal Court investigation in Kenya is an ongoing investigation into the responsibility for the 2007-2008 post-election violence.
The matter was referred to the International Criminal Court in The Hague.

topic.name: Christian and Hindu Philosophical Texts
topic.description: This cluster discusses the interpretations and philosophical implications of key religious texts and figures in Christianity and Hinduism.

Document 1: Christian interpretations of Virgil's Eclogue 4
early Christians...reinterpreted the poem to be about the birth of Jesus Christ.
Document 3: Christian meditation is a form of prayer
aims to heighten the personal relationship based on the love of God.
Document 4: Maitreyi was a Hindu philosopher
explored the Hindu concept of Atman in a dialogue with Yajnavalkya.
Document 9: The Devi Upanishad...is one of the minor Upanishads of Hinduism
the Goddess is the Brahman (ultimate metaphysical Reality).
Document 10: The Kaivalya Upanishad...is an ancient Sanskrit text
discussing Atman (Soul, Self) and its relation to Brahman.

topic.name: Atlantic Hurricane Seasons
topic.description: The documents discuss various Atlantic hurricane seasons, detailing the formation, activity levels, and impacts of tropical cyclones during those years.

Document 1: The 1997 Atlantic hurricane season was a below average season
The season officially began on June 1, and lasted until November 30.
Document 2: The 2000 Atlantic hurricane season was the first Atlantic hurricane season without a tropical cyclone in the month of July since 1993
It was slightly above average due to a La Niña weather pattern.
Document 5: The 1980 Atlantic hurricane season was tied with 1932, 1969, and 1994 for most named storms in Atlantic Ocean during the month of November
The season officially began on June 1, 1980, and lasted until November 30, 1980.
Document 4: Tropical Storm Arthur was the first tropical cyclone of the 2002 Atlantic hurricane season
The origins of the storm are believed to have been from a decaying cold front in the Gulf of Mexico.

topic.description: A collection of detailed summaries of various motorsport races, including Formula One and NASCAR events.

Document 8: 2011 Kobalt Tools 400 was a NASCAR Sprint Cup Series stock car race held on March 6, 2011
Carl Edwards, driving for Roush Fenway Racing, won the race

topic.name: The Boat Race History
topic.description: The documents discuss the annual rowing competition known as The Boat Race between the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge, detailing its historical significance, results, and notable events.

Document 1: The 47th Boat Race took place in 1890.
The race was first held in 1829.
Document 2: The 65th Boat Race took place on 4 April 1908.
Cambridge were reigning champions, having won the previous year's race.
Document 3: The 46th Boat Race took place on 30 March 1889.
Cambridge won by three lengths in a time of 20 minutes 14 seconds.
Document 4: The 50th Boat Race took place on 22 March 1893.
Oxford won by a length and a quarter in a time of 18 minutes 45 seconds.
Document 5: The 115th Boat Race took place on 5 April 1969.
It was won by Cambridge who passed the finishing post four lengths ahead of Oxford.
Document 6: The 73rd Boat Race took place on 30 March 1921.
Cambridge won by one length in a time of 19 minutes 45 seconds.
Document 7: The 117th Boat Race took place on 27 March 1971.
It was won by Cambridge who passed the finishing post ten lengths ahead of Oxford.
Document 8: The 35th Boat Race took place on 13 April 1878.
Oxford won by a margin of 10 lengths in a time of 22 minutes 15 seconds.
Document 9: The 74th Boat Race took place on 1 April 1922.
Cambridge won by four-and-a-half lengths in a time of 19 minutes 27 seconds.
Document 10: The 63rd Boat Race took place on 7 April 1906.
Cambridge won by three-and-a-half lengths in a time of 19 minutes 25 seconds.

topic.name: Medieval Churches in Anglesey
topic.description: The documents discuss various medieval churches located in Anglesey, Wales, highlighting their historical significance, architectural features, and current status.

Document 1: St Michael's Church is a former parish church in Anglesey, Wales, which is now closed and in ruins.
It is a Grade II listed building, a national designation given to 'buildings of special interest, which warrant every effort being made to preserve them'.
Document 2: St Edern's Church is a medieval parish church in the village of Bodedern, in Anglesey, north Wales.
It is a Grade II * listed building, a national designation given to 'particularly important buildings of more than special interest'.
Document 3: St Mary's Church is a medieval church near Aberffraw in Anglesey, north Wales.
It is a Grade I listed building, a national designation given to buildings of 'exceptional, usually national, interest'.
Document 4: St Eugrad's Church is an isolated church near the village of Marian-glas, in Anglesey, north Wales.
St Eugrad's is a Grade II * listed building, a national designation given to 'particularly important buildings of more than special interest'.
Document 5: St Mary's Church, Bodewryd is a small medieval church in the hamlet of Bodewryd, in Anglesey, north Wales.
It is a Grade II listed building, a national designation given to 'buildings of special interest, which warrant every effort being made to preserve them'.
Document 6: St Peter's Church is a redundant Anglican church in the town of Sudbury, Suffolk, England.
It is recorded in the National Heritage List for England as a designated Grade I listed building.
Document 7: St Peter's Church, Llanbedrgoch, is a small medieval parish church near the village of Llanbedrgoch in Anglesey, north Wales.
It is a Grade II * listed building, a national designation given to 'particularly important buildings of more than special interest'.
Document 8: St Fflewin's Church is a small rural church, situated by a farm in Anglesey, north Wales.
It is a Grade II listed building, a national designation given to 'buildings of special interest, which warrant every effort being made to preserve them'.
Document 9: St Mary's Church, Rhodogeidio is a small medieval church, dating from the 15th century, near Llannerch-y-medd, in Anglesey, north Wales.
It is a Grade II listed building, a national designation given to 'buildings of special interest, which warrant every effort being made to preserve them'.
Document 10: St Beuno's Church is a 12th-century parish church in Anglesey, north Wales.
St Beuno's is a Grade II * listed building, a national designation given to 'particularly important buildings of more than special interest'.

topic.name: Islamic Military Campaigns
topic.description: The documents discuss various military engagements and sieges involving Islamic forces and their interactions with Byzantine and other empires during the early medieval period.

Document 1: Battle of Tawahin was fought in 885 between the Abbasid Caliphate and the Tulunid ruler of Egypt and Syria.
The battle ended with a Tulunid victory, reaffirming their control over Syria.
Document 2: The Muslim conquest of Sicily began in June 827 and lasted until 902.
This conquest was part of the Aghlabid emirs' expansion efforts.
Document 4: The Siege of Güns was a siege in 1532 where Habsburg forces defended against the Ottoman army.
The defenders prevented the advance of the Ottoman army toward Vienna.
Document 6: The Siege of Nicaea in 727 was an unsuccessful attempt by the Umayyad Caliphate to capture the Byzantine city.
The successful repulsion of the attack was a major boost for Byzantine power.
Document 8: The Battle of Krasos occurred in 804 between the Byzantines and an Abbasid army.
Nikephoros I faced a heavy defeat during the Abbasid invasion.
Document 10: The Battle of Baykand was fought in 729 between the Turgesh khaganate and the Umayyad Caliphate.
The Arab army campaigned to suppress a rebellion supported by the Turgesh.

topic.name: Geographical Features and Water Bodies
topic.description: The documents collectively discuss various geographical features, including wilderness areas, rivers, creeks, and lakes, focusing on their ecological significance and historical context.

Document 1: Vätsäri Wilderness Area is located along the northeastern shore of Lake Inari in Inari, northern Finland.
The area has a short growing season, cold winters, little precipitation and poor soil quality.
Document 2: Yamsay Mountain is a large shield volcano in the Cascade Range of south-central Oregon.
The mountain offers activities such as hiking and horseback riding.
Document 3: Messers Run is a tributary of Catawissa Creek in Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania.
The creek has some alkalinity, but is slightly acidic.
Document 4: Shickshinny Creek is a tributary of the Susquehanna River in the Wyoming Valley in Luzerne County, Pennsylvania.
The creek is designated as a Coldwater Fishery and a Migratory Fishery.
Document 5: Harveys Lake is a natural lake in Luzerne County, Pennsylvania.
The lake is the largest natural lake in Pennsylvania by volume.
Document 6: Hunlock Creek is a tributary of the Susquehanna River in Luzerne County, Pennsylvania.
The watershed of the creek has an area of 32.5 square miles.
Document 7: The Oregon high desert is a region of the U.S. state of Oregon, located east of the Cascade Range.
Most of the high desert is not dry enough to truly qualify as desert.
Document 9: The Little Applegate River is a 21-mile-long tributary of the Applegate River located in the U.S. state of Oregon.
The watershed supports populations of coho and Chinook salmon.
Document 10: Solomon Creek is a tributary of the Susquehanna River in Luzerne County, Pennsylvania.
The creek was first settled by Native Americans around 8000 to 6000 B.C.E.

topic.name: Australian and New Zealand Infantry Battalions in WWII
topic.description: This topic covers the formation, deployment, and actions of various Australian and New Zealand infantry battalions during World War II.

Document 1: The 25th Battalion was an infantry battalion of the New Zealand Military Forces, which served during the Second World War
It participated in the Italian Campaign, fighting in actions at Orsogna and later at Cassino.
Document 2: The 23rd Battalion was an infantry battalion of the Australian Army.
It was raised in 1915 as part of the Australian Imperial Force for service during World War I.
Document 3: The 2/10th Battalion was an infantry battalion of the Australian Army that was raised for overseas service as part of the all volunteer Second Australian Imperial Force during World War II.
The battalion's first experience of combat came during an attack against the Italian garrison at Giarabub in March 1941.
Document 4: The 32nd Battalion was an infantry battalion of the Australian Army.
During the Second World War the battalion was merged with the 14th Battalion to become the 14th/32nd Battalion.
Document 5: The 26th Battalion was an infantry battalion of the Australian Army.
The battalion fought at Gallipoli in the latter stages of that campaign before being withdrawn to Egypt in late 1915.
Document 6: The 27th Battalion was an infantry battalion of the Australian Army.
During World War II the battalion was used mainly in a garrison role until the last year of the war.
Document 7: The 2/15th Battalion was an infantry battalion of the Australian Army that served during World War II.
After completing training in Palestine, in early 1941, the 2/15th took up positions along the front line in the Western Desert.
Document 9: The 2/31st Battalion was an infantry battalion of the Australian Army that served during World War II.
In late 1942 the battalion was sent to New Guinea to fight against the Japanese along the Kokoda Track.
Document 10: The 2/17th Battalion was an infantry battalion of the Australian Army.
In early 1944, the battalion was withdrawn to Australia for rest and reorganisation.

topic.name: French Ironclad Warships
topic.description: The documents discuss various French ironclad warships built in the late 19th century, focusing on their design, specifications, and naval operations.

Document 1: French ironclad Friedland was originally intended to be an iron-hulled version of Océan class armoured frigate built for the French Navy during the 1870s
supported the French occupation of Tunisia in 1881.
Document 2: The French ironclad Colbert was the lead ship of the Colbert-class ironclads that were built for the French Navy in the 1870s
took part in the French conquest of Tunisia, notably shelling and landing troops in Sfax on 15 – 16 July 1881.
Document 5: Océan was a wooden-hulled, armored frigate built for the French Navy in the mid to late 1860s
was commissioned in 1870 to participate in the Franco-Prussian War.

topic.name: WWE Pay-Per-View Events
topic.description: The documents discuss various professional wrestling pay-per-view events produced by World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE), highlighting match outcomes, feuds, and event details.

1: Cyber Sunday was the fourth annual Cyber Sunday professional wrestling pay-per-view event produced by World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE).
The main match on the SmackDown! brand was Batista versus The Undertaker for the World Heavyweight Championship.
3: Backlash (2004) was the sixth annual Backlash professional wrestling pay-per-view event produced by World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE).
The main event was a Triple Threat match for the World Heavyweight Championship involving reigning champion Chris Benoit, Triple H, and Shawn Michaels.
4: Unforgiven (2007) was the tenth annual Unforgiven pay-per-view event produced by World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE).
The main match on the SmackDown brand was The Undertaker versus Mark Henry.
5: The Great American Bash (2007) was the fourth annual Great American Bash professional wrestling pay-per-view event produced by World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE).
The main match on the Raw brand was John Cena versus Bobby Lashley for the WWE Championship.
8: Backlash (2007) was a professional wrestling pay-per-view event produced by World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE).
It was the ninth annual event under the Backlash name and starred talent from Raw, SmackDown!, and ECW.

topic.name: Notorious Historical Figures
topic.description: The documents discuss the lives and actions of various historical figures known for their controversial or infamous reputations.

Document 1: Jereboam O. Beauchamp was an American lawyer who murdered the Kentucky legislator Solomon P. Sharp.
The crime is known as the Beauchamp – Sharp Tragedy.
Document 2: Lorena Hickok was an American journalist known for her close relationship with First Lady Eleanor Roosevelt.
The exact nature of this relationship has been widely discussed by historians.
Document 3: Martha Wise was an American poisoner who retaliated by poisoning seventeen family members.
The case is considered one of the most sensational of the era in Ohio.
Document 4: Hedwig Potthast was the private secretary and mistress of Reichsführer-SS Heinrich Himmler.
She refrained from answering questions about Himmler 's involvement in war crimes.
Document 5: Ellen Sadler, known as The Sleeping Girl of Turville, purportedly fell asleep and did not wake for nine years.
The case attracted international attention from newspapers, medical professionals and the public.
Document 6: Lucy Mercer Rutherfurd is best known for her affair with US President Franklin D. Roosevelt.
The relationship was discovered by Eleanor in September 1918.
Document 7: Julia Baird is the younger half-sister of English musician John Lennon.
She is now a director of Cavern City Tours in Liverpool.
Document 8: Jane Williams was best known for her association with the Romantic poet Percy Bysshe Shelley.
She lived with Thomas Jefferson Hogg and had two children with him.
Document 9: Allegra Byron was the illegitimate daughter of the poet George Gordon, Lord Byron.
She was turned over to Byron when she was fifteen months-old.
Document 10: Francis Folger Franklin was the eldest son of Benjamin Franklin who died of smallpox.
His death devastated both his parents, who doted upon Francis.
